description_long: This apartment building consists of two connected structures, each with a steep, dark gray gabled roof and white facades. The buildings feature multiple rows of symmetrically arranged windows, with the lower floors having larger, arched windows and the upper floors displaying smaller, rectangular ones. Decorative elements such as window frames and signage add visual interest to the street-facing sides. The ground floor includes several arched entrances and commercial signage, suggesting mixed-use with retail or service spaces at street level. The overall form is blocky but softened by the rooflines and window arrangements, and the buildings are closely joined, forming a continuous street frontage. The architectural style hints at a blend of traditional European urban design with modernized details.
description: A pair of connected, white apartment buildings with steep dark gray roofs, arched ground-floor entrances, and rows of symmetrically arranged windows. The facade features commercial signage and decorative window frames, evoking a traditional European urban style.
description_view_0: The rear and rooflines of the two connected buildings are visible, showing the dark gray gabled roofs and the overall blocky structure.
description_view_1: This view highlights the side of one building with rows of windows, a stepped roofline, and visible commercial signage on the ground floor.
description_view_2: The front facades are shown, featuring arched entrances, commercial signage, and a symmetrical arrangement of windows across both buildings.
description_view_3: The opposite side and roof of the buildings are visible, with less detail on entrances but clear window arrangements and the overall massing.
attribute_window_arrangement: Windows are symmetrically arranged in horizontal rows, with larger arched windows on the ground floor and smaller rectangular windows on the upper floors. The pattern is regular and evenly spaced across both buildings.
